fde.title: Rubrik 宣布即将集成 Amazon Bedrock AgentCore 以保护 AI 智能体
fde.useCase: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore to Secure AI Agents
fde.reportedNeed: Cybersecurity leaders estimate that in the next year, half or more of attacks they face will be driven by agentic AI
deliveryComponents: Rubrik Announces Upcoming Integration with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore to Secure AI Agents

AWS Summit -- Rubrik (NYSE: RBRK), the Security and AI Operations company, announced today its upcoming Rubrik Agent Cloud (RAC) integration with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. The upcoming integration reflects Rubrik's commitment to extending the security controls customers already trust into agentic workflows.

"Speed has always been the enterprise mandate, but today, safety is the prerequisite," said Devvret Rishi, General Manager AI, Rubrik. "Cybersecurity leaders estimate that in the next year, half or more of attacks they face will be driven by agentic AI. Rubrik’s upcoming integration with AWS Bedrock AgentCore gives enterprises the confidence to deploy AI agents quickly with trustworthy governance."

fde.title: Anthropic CEO 称政府应有权阻止高风险新 AI 模型部署
fde.useCase: Anthropic CEO 称政府应有权阻止高风险新 AI 模型部署
fde.reportedNeed: AI models should undergo mandatory testing by third parties to assess the risk they pose across several categories, including enabling cybersecurity threats and biological weapons
deliveryComponents: Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei said the government should have the power to block artificial intelligence developers from deploying new AI models if they present certain risks.

In a lengthy essay on Wednesday, Amodei argued that AI models should undergo mandatory testing by third parties to assess the risk they pose across several categories, including enabling cybersecurity threats and biological weapons. If the AI is deemed to “present unacceptable risks,” he wrote, “the government should have the power to block or deter deployment.”
